Base connector class.
Connector objects for each supported transport protocol.
The connectors here are responsible for discovering and removing backend
storage target for each of the supported transport protocols.
exceptions)
	linuxscsi)
ExecutionTimeout)
execute_cmd)
ProcessExecutionErrorc
BaseConnectorc
	LinuxSCSI
self
)./fsm/device/initiator/connectors/base.py
__init__
BaseConnector.__init__c
        Connect to a storage target.
        The connection_properties describes the information needed by
        the specific protocol to use to make the connection.
        The connection_properties is a dictionary that describes the target.
        It varies slightly by protocol type (iscsi, fibre_channel),
        but the structure is usually the same.
        :param connection_properties: The dictionary that describes all
                                      of the target attributes.
        :type connection_properties: dict
        :returns: dict
        Nr
connection_propertiesr
connect_storage_backend"
z%BaseConnector.connect_storage_backendFc
        Disconnect a storage target from the local host.
        The connection_properties are the same as from connect_backend_storage.
        The device_info is returned from connect_backend_storage.
        :param connection_properties: The dictionary that describes all
                                      of the target attributes.
        :type connection_properties: dict
        :param device_info: historical difference, but same as connection_props
        :type device_info: dict
        :param force: Whether to forcefully disconnect even if flush fails.
        :type force: bool
        :param ignore_errors: When force is True, this will decide whether to
                              ignore errors or raise an exception once finished
                              the operation.  Default is False.
        :type ignore_errors: bool
        Nr
device_info
force
ignore_errorsr
disconnect_storage_backend5
z(BaseConnector.disconnect_storage_backendc
Return the directory where a Connector looks for luns.
        Now only supports where do we look for iSCSI based luns and FC
        based luns. In future if support another type, could define this
        method to be an abstract method.
        z
/dev/disk/by-pathr
get_search_pathL
BaseConnector.get_search_pathc
        Test to see if the device path is a real device.
        :param path: The file system path for the device.
        :type path: str
        :returns: bool
        Z

check_valid_deviceU
z BaseConnector.check_valid_devicec
This method discovers a multipath device.
        Discover a multipath device based on a defined connection_property
        and a device_wwn and return the multipath_id and path of the multipath
        enabled device if there is one.
        NZ
devicezLUnable to find multipath device name for lun. Using path %(device)s for lun.Z

roz6Block device %s is still read-only. Continuing anyway.)
